name: CAIR FOUNDATION INC
mission: Communications Department works in conjunction with local and national media to ensure an accurate portrayal of Islam and Muslims is presented to the American public.
theoryOfChange: CAIR Foundation has published a '2024 General Election CAIR's Strategic Election Plan' which outlines specific goals and actions to achieve them, such as increasing Muslim voter registration, educating on policy, advocating for peace in Palestine, and fighting Islamophobia. A similar document, 'CAIR'S STRATEGIC ELECTION PLAN,' details electoral programs and goals for the 2020 and 2022 elections. Furthermore, Fahrenheit Advisors facilitated CAIR's strategic planning process and created a scorecard of new performance metrics, indicating a documented approach to measuring change. Charity Navigator also highlights CAIR's mission and goals related to advocacy, civil rights, and community empowerment, suggesting an underlying strategic framework for impact and measurement.
scoreSummary: CAIR FOUNDATION INC shows strong impact and good alignment, with zakat compliance.

evaluationSources: Congressional Research Service (2010 report) cited CAIR's cooperation with authorities

zakatClaimEvidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://www.cair.com/zakat (Source: https://www.cair.com/zakat)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: CEO compensation is 2.5% of revenue ($236,198 on $9,379,629)
keyConcerns.detail: For orgs with revenue $5-50M, CEO pay above 2% of revenue warrants scrutiny.

components.improvement_suggestion: Clarify zakat allocation policy and eligible use categories for donors.

dimension_explanations.alignment: As a Zakat-eligible organization, it provides a medium fit for Muslim donors seeking to support civil rights and religious representation [2]. The cause is considered urgent due to its focus on defending the constitutional rights of American Muslims [4][5].
dimension_explanations.impact: The foundation demonstrates high organizational capacity with a program expense ratio of 66.4% and significant working capital of 11.4 months [1][3]. While it effectively maintains operations and media presence, its impact is primarily categorized as capacity building within the advocacy sector [4].

strengths: High financial accountability with a Charity Navigator score of 98.33333333333333/100
Strong fundraising efficiency of $0.16 per $1 raised
Robust liquidity with 11.4 months of working capital
baseline_narrative.summary: The CAIR Foundation Inc. operates as a prominent civil rights and advocacy organization dedicated to ensuring an accurate portrayal of Islam and Muslims in the United States [4]. With a total revenue of $9,379,629, the organization manages programs focused on research, media relations, and legal defense [1][3].

areas_for_improvement.detail: While the organization reports a <cite id="3">program expense ratio of 66.4%</cite> in some filings, Charity Navigator's analysis places it at <cite id="15">66.4%</cite>, which is lower than some service-based peers.
The organization currently tracks <cite id="16">4 outcome metrics</cite> via Candid; increasing this would provide clearer evidence of cost-effectiveness.

alignment.improvement: The organization could <cite id="9">clarify its zakat allocation policy</cite> and specific eligible use categories to better guide donors.

credibility.improvement: Credibility could be further enhanced by publishing more granular <cite id="16">beneficiary data</cite> to allow for cost-per-outcome analysis.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize systemic civil rights defense and want to fulfill zakat through long-term advocacy for the American Muslim community.
donor_motivations: Want to protect the civil liberties of Muslims in the US
Value media representation and accurate portrayals of Islam
Prefer investing in institutional infrastructure over short-term relief
giving_considerations: Advocacy impact is often long-term and harder to quantify than direct aid
A portion of funds supports legal and administrative overhead to maintain high accountability
Contributions support national-level policy and media influence
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking immediate, individual-level humanitarian relief or those who prefer charities with 90%+ program expense ratios.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects verified outcome tracking through Candid but a lack of independent experimental evaluations (RCTs).
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: CAIR creates impact by providing legal counsel to victims of discrimination, lobbying for policy change, and training community members to engage in advocacy.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence in advocacy ensures that legal and media strategies are actually resulting in policy shifts and improved public perception.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: As a national advocacy body, CAIR has a high capacity to absorb funds to expand its legal and government affairs departments.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its <cite id="1">32-year history</cite> and <cite id="12">resilient 11.9-month capital reserve</cite>.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a <cite id="6">98.33/100 rating from Charity Navigator</cite>, supported by a <cite id="10">98/100 score in Accountability & Finance</cite> and a board with <cite id="11">88% independent members</cite>.
CAIR possesses <cite id="12">11.4 months of working capital</cite>, providing significant stability to sustain long-term legal and advocacy campaigns.
The foundation spends only <cite id="13">$0.19 to raise every $1</cite>, significantly outperforming many advocacy-sector peers.

strengths_deep_dive: CAIR's institutional strength is rooted in its long-term track record and transparency. Having operated for <cite id="1">32 years</cite>, it has developed a sophisticated infrastructure for civil rights defense. Its financial health is evidenced by a <cite id="14">3-year revenue CAGR of 13.6%</cite>, indicating steady growth in donor trust. Furthermore, the organization's commitment to transparency is verified by its <cite id="5">Candid Platinum Seal</cite>, the highest level of recognition for non-profit data sharing.
rich_narrative.summary: Founded in <cite id="1">1994</cite>, the CAIR Foundation Inc. has established itself as the nation's largest Muslim civil rights and advocacy organization. Its mission centers on <cite id="2">enhancing the understanding of Islam</cite>, protecting civil liberties, and empowering American Muslims through legal defense and media representation. The organization operates with a robust financial base, reporting <cite id="3">total revenue of $7,942,722</cite> in the 2023 tax year. CAIR's work is structured around five core pillars: <cite id="4">civil rights, media relations, government affairs, education, and community empowerment</cite>. By maintaining a <cite id="5">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite> and an <cite id="6">overall score of 98.98.33333333333333/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>, the foundation demonstrates a high level of institutional maturity and public accountability. Their legal department actively challenges discriminatory policies, recently securing <cite id="7">court rulings striking down discriminatory laws</cite>, while their communications team works to ensure an <cite id="8">accurate portrayal of Islam</cite> in national media. For donors, CAIR offers a unique vehicle for <cite id="9">zakat-eligible advocacy</cite>, specifically targeting the 'fisabilillah' category to protect the communal rights of Muslims in the United States.
